target.name: Deterrence
target.founder: Dhruva Rajendra, Brian Jones, Henry Olgers

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $29.0B.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Deterrence is a Seed-stage defense & security company that has raised $10M+. Valuation analysis draws on a peer universe of 10 comparable companies—2 public and 8 private—spanning early to growth stages, including Scale AI, Stark Defence, Vatn Systems, Ares Industries, and Tenna Systems.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression methodology uses the $10M total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $25M. Peer-median analysis across the 5-company comp set (range: $60M to $29.0B) produces a median implied valuation of $300M, though this set exhibits wide dispersion. The peer methodology is constrained by stage and sector heterogeneity—the median is heavily influenced by later-stage Series A companies, including one outlier at $29B.

The triangulation across both methods supports a valuation range of $21M to $345M, with a midpoint of $162M. The stage-progression method anchors the lower bound conservatively; the peer median anchors the upper bound but carries material uncertainty given the stage mismatch between Deterrence (Seed) and most comparables (Series A). Key risks to valuation include the limited visibility into Deterrence's revenue, customer traction, and competitive positioning relative to the defense-tech peer set. The wide peer range reflects fundamental uncertainty about frontier-tech defensibility and market adoption trajectories in this sector.
